summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a fiery 1992 Hamilton show highlighted by a soaring Dark Star, blistering Shakedown Street and an epic The Other One.
review: The recording is a super‑clean SBD that preserves every nuance of the band while missing only a bit of crowd ambience.  Musically the night shines in the second set – Dark Star unfolds with jazzy depth, Shakedown Street erupts with Jerry’s incendiary solo and The Other One rolls on forever, all backed by a thumping Phil bass line and lively keyboards.  Overall fans rank it among the top 1992 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, the vast majority are 4‑5 stars and praise both the performance (especially Dark Star, Shakedown Street, The Other One) and the crystal‑clear soundboard source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear, super‑clean mix with excellent instrument separation; a few reviewers note the lack of audience ambience and wish for an AUD supplement.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set described as fluid, wave‑y and ‘cooking’, with jams that build organically.  First set is solid (The Same Thing, Althea) but generally seen as a warm‑up to the spectacular second half.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solos on Shakedown Street and Dark Star are repeatedly called ‘ridiculously good’ and his vocal phrasing noted as spot‑on.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is praised as a “living organism” that stomps through the set without getting thin.
band_member_comments.Keys: Debate over keyboards – Vince Welnick’s playing is mentioned positively, while some lament the loss of Brent Mydland’s Hammond funk; Bruce Hornsby appears on later shows but not this night.
